comment.body:
SP:SPX (S&P Index): This is the official index. Since the index itself doesn't trade, it technically has zero volume. If you apply a Volume Profile here and see data, TradingView is likely aggregating the volume of the 500 underlying stocks (though this is often criticized for being less "clean" than futures data).
TVC:SPX (TradingView Computer): This is TradingView’s own calculation. The "volume" here is often derived from a composite of various data feeds they receive, rather than a single exchange's order book.
CFD Providers (e.g., OANDA:SPX500, SAXO:SPX): If you are looking at a CFD version of the SPX, the volume profile is based only on that specific broker's volume. This represents only a tiny fraction of the global market and is generally considered unreliable for institutional-level analysis.

comment.body:
let me tell you why...
the reason usually is the negligation of probabilities.
i know there are high expectations regarding me.
but as said once earlier:
if my analyses have 70% accuracy rate (regarding the levels, pivot zones, probability bias) it still means that there will be 3 and a half month (bit more than one quarter) when i'll be inaccurate.
even if my analyses are 90% accurate, thats still roughly two and a half month inaccuracy! in a year.
but in reality accuracy rate is also fluctuating.
it's up to you how you manage the risk of ruin.

i found a model that has the best edge available for me now. in terms of momentum trading.
i know how accurate my levels and projected distributions are, and you all experience it. 
will it always be accurate? hell no... but the key is that it is not my fault, or yours. simply this is how the system works. Thats why risk management is key. You got edu posts on that.

this is why experienced traders don't like negativity against the daily posts.
bcs there is a difference between being critical against my methodology or interpretation, or hauling me up for the fluctuating accuracy!
the first would be reasonable if there would be a statistically significant amount of occassion when my levels didn't play out, or did the opposite, dynamics were completely different etc-etc. if you feel so, backtest the year :)) posts are there untouched.
while the second one is in fact a natural phenomenon that must be accepted. overemphatizing only one side distorting the truth is unacceptable.

...think about it...
